sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board changed after RIGHT action. Let me compare the before and after frames to see what moved. I need to check the transition to understand what the RIGHT action did.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after the RIGHT action
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

before_seg = before.segmentation
after_seg = after.segmentation

before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before_seg['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after_seg['nodes']}

# Check which objects changed position by comparing boundaries
def node_center(n):
    b = n['boundary']
    return (sum(p[0] for p in b)/len(b), sum(p[1] for p in b)/len(b))

print("Objects that moved (center changed):")
for nid in before_nodes:
    if nid in after_nodes:
        bc = node_center(before_nodes[nid])
        ac = node_center(after_nodes[nid])
        if abs(bc[0]-ac[0]) > 0.5 or abs(bc[1]-ac[1]) > 0.5:
            print(f"  id={nid} color={before_nodes[nid]['color']} px={before_nodes[nid]['pixels']} {bc} -> {ac}")

# Also check for new/disappeared nodes
before_ids = set(before_nodes.keys())
after_ids = set(after_nodes.keys())
print(f"\nNew nodes: {after_ids - before_ids}")
print(f"Gone nodes: {before_ids - after_ids}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

- Node 38 (dark gray, 576px) - this is the bottom border bar, it shifted

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Test more movements to understand control
# The gray+green block (id=32/34) seems to be the player piece
# Let me try moving it more to understand the goal
# First, let's see the current position of the player piece
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

player = [n for n in nodes if n['id'] == 32][0]
print(f"Player (id=32) center: {player['boundary']}")

# Try moving RIGHT a few more times to see if it reaches the divider
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
